mission: Synthesize findings from all agents and validate the proposed metadata fix

root_cause_synthesis.problem: Converted data from `_db_processed_views` is being incorrectly identified as legacy data requiring coordinate correction, causing double-transformation when the refresh button is used

trigger_mechanism.step_1: PHP backend converts `_db_processed_views` to canvas format via `convert_processed_views_to_canvas_data()` (Line 6501)
trigger_mechanism.step_3: JavaScript frontend receives converted data and detects `metadata.source === 'db_processed_views'` (Line 578)
trigger_mechanism.step_4: Frontend classifies data as 'legacy_db' format based on this marker
trigger_mechanism.step_5: Legacy correction layer applies +80px vertical shift and 1.23× scale correction (Lines 1075-1198)
trigger_mechanism.step_6: Data that was already correctly converted gets incorrectly transformed again
trigger_mechanism.root_issue: The metadata marker 'db_processed_views' is being used for TWO different purposes: (1) to indicate data needs conversion from print-DB format, and (2) to indicate data is legacy and needs coordinate correction. These are NOT the same thing.

impact.visual_symptom: Elements render at wrong positions and scales after conversion
impact.affected_workflow: Order detail pages with 'Refresh Design Preview' button
impact.affected_orders: Any order with data stored in `_db_processed_views` format (legacy print database format)
impact.severity: HIGH - Causes incorrect visual rendering for legacy orders

current_metadata.purpose: Indicates data was converted from print-DB format

proposed_fix.description: Change metadata.source value from 'db_processed_views' to 'converted_from_processed_views' and add modern metadata markers

reasoning.detection_priority: Frontend detection checks metadata.source === 'db_processed_views' first (Line 578) before checking missing metadata fields
reasoning.new_behavior: With source='converted_from_processed_views', the explicit legacy marker check fails
reasoning.fallback_check: The fallback check (missing capture_version && missing designer_offset) also fails because we now add these fields
reasoning.classification_result: Data will be classified as 'modern' format (Line 597), skipping all legacy corrections
reasoning.proof: Lines 660-670 show legacy corrections are only applied when isLegacyDbFormat=true OR both metadata fields are missing. Both conditions become false with the fix.

true_legacy_data.source: Original _db_processed_views data that has NOT been converted yet
true_legacy_data.conversion: PHP converts it via convert_processed_views_to_canvas_data() with NEW metadata
true_legacy_data.frontend_treatment: Receives already-corrected data with modern metadata markers
true_legacy_data.result: No double-correction - data is converted once in PHP with correct coordinates

converted_data.source: Data that was previously converted and cached
converted_data.treatment: Classified as 'modern' format, rendered 1:1 without corrections

side_effects.effect: Converted data will be treated as modern format

side_effects.impact: NEUTRAL - Backend conversion still happens as before

side_effects.effect: Adds capture_version and designer_offset to metadata
side_effects.impact: POSITIVE - Makes converted data self-documenting and compatible with modern systems

side_effects.effect: Historical orders with old 'db_processed_views' marker will still get legacy correction
side_effects.impact: EXPECTED - Maintains backward compatibility for unconverted cached data

system_architecture.alignment: ✅ Fix maintains documented conversion flow, only changes metadata markers

phase3_deployment.alignment: ✅ Fix follows Phase 3 pattern of using metadata for format detection

agent_reports.alignment: ⚠️ PARTIAL - Proposed fix is simpler (metadata change) vs full data adapter, but achieves same goal

alternative_solutions.description: Create normalizeDesignData() function that converts all formats to canonical structure before rendering
alternative_solutions.implementation: Add ~200 lines of JavaScript normalization code in frontend
pros: Single entry point for all conversions
Easy to add new formats in future
cons: Requires extensive testing of all format combinations
Overkill for single metadata marker issue

alternative_solutions.description: Add explicit check to distinguish between 'needs conversion' vs 'needs correction'
alternative_solutions.implementation: Modify classifyDataFormat() to check metadata.conversion_complete flag
pros: Surgical fix with minimal code changes

alternative_solutions.description: Change metadata.source to unique value and add modern metadata markers
cons: Requires cache flush for existing converted data
Old cached data with 'db_processed_views' marker will still trigger legacy correction (acceptable - will auto-fix on next conversion)

reasoning: SIMPLICITY: 3-line code change vs 200+ lines for data adapter
EFFECTIVENESS: Completely prevents legacy correction for converted data
MAINTAINABILITY: Self-documenting metadata is clearer than implicit markers
SAFETY: No changes to frontend logic = no risk of regression
PERFORMANCE: Zero overhead vs 2-5ms for data adapter approach
STANDARDS: Aligns with modern format metadata structure (capture_version, designer_offset)
BACKWARD COMPATIBILITY: True legacy data flow unchanged

verification: Check browser console for 'Format = MODERN' (not LEGACY_DB)
Verify no +80px offset or 1.23× scale applied

implementation_steps.note: Forces re-conversion of cached data with new metadata

testing_requirements.verification: Console shows 'Format = MODERN', no legacy correction applied

testing_requirements.orders: Any order with unconverted _db_processed_views in database
testing_requirements.expected: PHP converts data once with correct coordinates + new metadata
No change in behavior - still classified as modern
testing_requirements.verification: Regression test to ensure no side effects

testing_requirements.orders: Cached data with old 'db_processed_views' marker (if any exist)
testing_requirements.expected: Still gets legacy correction until cache expires or is cleared
testing_requirements.verification: Acceptable temporary behavior - auto-fixes on next conversion

confidence_reasoning: Root cause precisely identified through code analysis
Fix mechanism validated against frontend detection logic
No frontend code changes = minimal regression risk
Backward compatibility maintained for true legacy data
3-line change is easy to review and understand
Rollback plan is simple and fast
Testing strategy covers all affected code paths

case_1_fresh_conversion.scenario: Order with _db_processed_views is accessed for first time after fix
case_1_fresh_conversion.behavior: PHP converts with new metadata (source='converted_from_processed_views', capture_version='3.0')

case_2_cached_old_metadata.scenario: Cached conversion data still has old 'db_processed_views' marker
case_2_cached_old_metadata.behavior: Frontend still classifies as 'legacy_db' and applies corrections
case_2_cached_old_metadata.impact: Temporary issue until cache expires or is manually flushed
case_2_cached_old_metadata.mitigation: Cache TTL is 5 minutes (documented), auto-fixes quickly

case_3_modern_orders.behavior: No change - data already has modern metadata structure

case_4_unconverted_legacy.scenario: Very old order with raw _db_processed_views, never converted
case_4_unconverted_legacy.behavior: PHP converts with new metadata, frontend treats as modern
case_4_unconverted_legacy.result: ✅ CORRECT - Coordinates corrected once during conversion

risk_assessment: VERY LOW - Surgical fix with clear rollback path

summary.problem_root_cause: Ambiguous metadata marker 'db_processed_views' used for both conversion indication and legacy correction trigger
summary.proposed_solution: Change metadata.source to 'converted_from_processed_views' and add modern metadata markers (capture_version, designer_offset)
summary.why_it_works: Frontend detection logic checks explicit marker first, then falls back to missing metadata check. New marker + added fields make both checks fail, preventing legacy correction.
summary.implementation_complexity: VERY SIMPLE - 3 lines changed in 1 file
summary.testing_complexity: LOW - 4 test cases covering all scenarios
summary.regression_risk: VERY LOW - No frontend changes, backend change is isolated to metadata
summary.final_recommendation: IMPLEMENT IMMEDIATELY - Simplest, safest, most effective solution
